body:
As a general rule of thumb, you should have your garage door professionally serviced **once a year**.

While Pleasanton doesn't have to deal with intense coastal salt air or freezing winter blizzards, our hot Tri-Valley summers and chilly, damp winters can cause metal components to expand, contract, and lose their lubrication over time.

Here is a breakdown of how often your door needs attention based on how you use it:

### The Maintenance Schedule

  * **Once a Year (Professional Tune-Up):** Highly recommended for the average Pleasanton home. A technician will inspect high-tension springs, check cables for fraying, balance the door, and ensure safety sensors are perfectly aligned. [Garage Door Maintenance Guide | Service & Warning Signs](https://www.doormn.com/blog/how-often-should-garage-door-be-serviced/#:~:text=The%20general%20recommendation%20is%20to,hinges%2C%20tracks%20and%20safety%20sensors.)

  * **Every 6 Months (High-Traffic Garages):** If your garage door acts as the main "front door" for your family, kids, and cars (opening and closing 4+ times a day), it racks up cycles quickly. Bi-annual service helps catch worn rollers or fatigued springs before they snap.

  * **Every 3 to 6 Months (DIY Quick Check):** You don't always need a pro. A few times a year, you should grab a can of specialized garage door lubricant (silicone or lithium spray—not WD-40) and spray down the rollers, hinges, and springs.

### ⚠️ Warning Signs You Need Service Sooner

Don’t wait for your annual check-up if you notice any of these Tri-Valley red flags:

  * **The "Pleasanton Screech":** Grinding, squeaking, or scraping noises usually mean dry bearings or worn-out rollers. [How Often Should Garage Doors Be Serviced and Why: Essential Tips for Homeowners](https://www.columbusdoorsales.com/blog/how-often-should-garage-doors-be-serviced-and-why-essential-tips-for-homeowners#:~:text=A%20tailored%20service%20schedule%20helps,or%20an%20imbalanced%20spring%20system.)

  * **Jerky or Slow Movement:** If the door struggles to lift or moves unevenly, the torsion springs are likely losing tension or the tracks are misaligned.

  * **Reversing Randomly:** If the door starts going down and pops right back up, the safety eyes might be misaligned or the internal force settings need adjusting.

> **Pro Tip:** Skipping regular maintenance can actually void the manufacturer's warranty on newer garage doors and openers, so keeping up with a quick yearly visit is usually worth the peace of mind. [How Often Should Garage Doors Be Serviced and Why: Essential Tips for Homeowners](https://www.columbusdoorsales.com/blog/how-often-should-garage-doors-be-serviced-and-why-essential-tips-for-homeowners)

How old is your current garage door system, and has it started making any unusual noises lately?
